description_long: This house features a simple, rectangular design with two main volumes, one larger and one smaller, both topped with dark gray gable roofs. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored horizontal siding, and the base is finished with a stone or concrete foundation. The property is surrounded by a patch of grass, and there are neatly trimmed bushes along the front and sides of the house. The structure lacks visible windows or doors, giving it a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The rooflines are clean and uninterrupted, and the landscaping is modest, enhancing the house's straightforward suburban character.
description: A light-colored, gable-roofed house with two main volumes, stone foundation, and minimal landscaping with bushes and grass.
description_view_0: Side view showing both the larger and smaller house volumes, with bushes nestled between them and a grassy yard.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical arrangement of the two side volumes, bushes along the facade, and the central main structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the rear and side of the house, with the two volumes and a grassy yard visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the house, both volumes, and the continuous gable roofs.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway not visible; facade is plain with bushes and no decorative elements.
